reforms.description: The national recovery plan includes measures to increase R&D funding with a focus on digitalisation, sustainability, and health, aiming to enhance innovation capacity across sectors. Additionally, Flanders, Wallonia, and the Brussels-Capital region have conducted reviews and improvements to their R&D subsidy schemes to enhance their effectiveness and streamline support measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity, which supports economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put into effect as part of the national recovery plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in R&D funding mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: In 2021, Flanders conduced a review of the effectiveness and additionality of its R&D subsidy scheme, Wallonia simplified and streamlined its R&D support measures and the Brussels-Capital region improved the mix of its R&D funding tools.

reforms.description: The recovery plans across regions include significant investments in digital education to align skills with labor market needs, addressing skill shortages in key sectors. Additionally, the federal government’s budget agreement to implement an individual training account transforms the obligation for firms to grant training days into an individual right for workers, enhancing access to training for all, including vulnerable groups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in digital education and training access is expected to improve the skill set of the workforce, which supports economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives have been enacted as part of the recovery plans.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a major overhaul in how training and education are provided to align with market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government’s budget agreement to implement an individual training account... is a major step in the direction of increasing lifelong learning efficiency and inclusiveness.

reforms.description: The national recovery plan allocates EUR 79 million to develop protection tools and a cybersecurity competence hub, aimed at enhancing digital security capabilities, particularly for SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing cybersecurity capabilities is crucial for the growth and sustainability of SMEs, which contributes to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been allocated and the initiative is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The allocation of funds for cybersecurity represents a significant but narrower reform focused on enhancing digital security.
reforms.source_quote: The national recovery plan allocates EUR 79 million to develop protection tools and a cybersecurity competence hub.

reforms.description: The National Plan for Fixed and Mobile Broadband aims for high-quality broadband connectivity with a contract speed of 100 Mbps by 2025 and 1 Gbps by 2030 for all households.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband connectivity is essential for supporting innovation and economic growth across all sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan is currently being executed as part of the national strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant investment in infrastructure that supports digital innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The National Plan for Fixed and Mobile Broadband aims for high-quality broadband connectivity with a contract speed of 100 Mbps by 2025 and 1 Gbps by 2030.
